blockingIssues.description: `Files::create` serializes `expires_after` as a JSON string multipart field instead of the required bracketed multipart fields (`expires_after[anchor]` and `expires_after[seconds]`), so callers using expiration would send the wrong wire shape.

`UploadCreateParams` and `UploadChunkedParams` reuse `FilePurpose`, exposing purposes such as `user_data` and `evals` even though the current Uploads API only documents `assistants`, `batch`, `fine-tune`, and `vision` as valid upload purposes.

`VectorStoreFiles::upload` uploads through `Files::create(...)` before validating `vector_store_id`, so a blank or invalid vector-store id can still create an orphaned file before the helper returns a local validation error; `upload_and_poll` inherits the same side effect.
`VectorStoreFileBatches::upload_and_poll` uploads new files sequentially with a plain `for` loop, but VAL-FILES-013 requires concurrent uploads before batch creation and polling.

nonBlockingIssues.description: `UploadStatus` has no unknown fallback, so additive server statuses would currently become parse failures instead of preserving forward compatibility.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented that Files and Uploads expose `expires_after` through different wire shapes so workers do not reuse the wrong serializer across the two surfaces.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the `sdk-surface-worker` requirement for `brainstorming` versus an equivalent design-note artifact when adding new public surface ergonomics, including where that evidence should appear in the transcript or handoff.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `webhook-verification-helpers` review found the public webhook surface matched the code contract but its transcript skeleton and handoff did not capture the `brainstorming` or chosen-vs-rejected design note that the skill currently requires.
